title: alloc_subscription_address advances diversifier before invoice persisted — diversifier skipped on count_and_create_invoice cap-reject
description: alloc_subscription_address() calls next_diversifier() and advances the counter unconditionally, then count_and_create_invoice() is called. If count_and_create_invoice returns Ok(false) (cap reached) or Err, the allocated diversifier address is discarded but the diversifier index has already been permanently bumped. Over many cap-rejected requests the diversifier counter advances without any corresponding invoice, leaking diversifier space. While 2^88 indices are effectively infinite, the pattern is a correctness gap: a Sapling address is permanently reserved with no invoice and no scan record, so any payment to it is silently ignored by the payment watcher (no invoice row to match). Trigger: client with ≥5 pending invoices sends additional SUBSCRIBE_REQUEST calls repeatedly.
